title: Save crashes with encoding error when task list contains CSV-imported data (macOS desktop)
problem: TaskFlow desktop app crashes (force-closes) every time the user saves a task list that contains tasks imported from a CSV file. A brief error dialog mentioning 'encoding' flashes before the app closes. The user loses unsaved work on every attempt. Removing the imported tasks restores save functionality.
root_cause_hypothesis: The CSV import path accepts text in an encoding (e.g., Latin-1, Windows-1252, or mixed/malformed UTF-8) that the save/serialization path cannot handle. When the save routine attempts to encode or serialize the task data, it encounters characters it cannot process and crashes instead of handling the error gracefully. The import codepath likely needs encoding normalization, and the save codepath needs a try/catch around serialization.
reproduction_steps: Import tasks from a CSV file containing non-ASCII or mixed-encoding characters (e.g., Latin-1 encoded text, accented characters, or BOM markers)
Verify the tasks appear in the task list
Observe: app crashes with a brief encoding-related error dialog
environment: macOS, TaskFlow desktop v2.3.1 (approximate), task list with ~200 tasks including CSV-imported data
impact: User is completely unable to save any task list that contains CSV-imported data. Workaround (deleting imported tasks) is not viable because the user needs those tasks. Any user who has imported CSV data with non-standard encoding is likely affected. Data loss occurs on every save attempt.
recommended_fix: 1. Add encoding detection/normalization to the CSV import path (e.g., use chardet or similar to detect source encoding and transcode to UTF-8 on import). 2. Add error handling around the save serialization path so encoding failures surface as user-visible errors rather than crashes. 3. Consider a migration or repair tool that re-encodes existing imported task data in-place for users already affected.
proposed_test_case: Import a CSV file containing text in Windows-1252 encoding (with characters like curly quotes, em-dashes, accented characters) into a task list, then save. Verify the save completes without error and the task data round-trips correctly. Also test with a CSV containing a mix of UTF-8 and Latin-1 encoded rows.
information_gaps: Exact error message from the crash dialog (flashes too fast for user to read — check application crash logs or stderr)
The original CSV file contents and encoding (user no longer has certainty about its contents)
